humanSummary.text: Final re-audit found no P0/P1 blockers. Candidate catalog ids now include work evidence subject ids, catalogRecordId filtering finds those candidates, and exact-link provenance is preserved. A non-blocking API schema weakness remains for enum-backed catalog conflict fields.

findings.title: Catalog conflict API schema accepts arbitrary enum strings
findings.description: The catalog conflict review API response schema validates required fields, but enum-backed fields such as status, conflictKind, catalogSource, externalIdKind, and sourceRecordKind are still accepted as arbitrary non-empty strings.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit confirmed repository output is enum-backed, but the API response validator would not catch a bad service, mock, or regression that returned an arbitrary string for these fields.
DB-backed catalog-conflict-review and authorization-matrix tests passed after the P1 repairs, and pnpm exec vp run ts:typecheck passed.

findings.impact: The read model currently emits constrained values, so CATALOG-010 can complete, but the API contract guard is weaker than the repository model and could allow future drift through tests or mocks.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: The catalog conflict API response schema validates status, conflictKind, catalogSource, externalIdKind, and sourceRecordKind against their canonical enum values.
A response fixture with arbitrary strings for those fields fails schema validation.
Existing valid conflict review API fixtures continue to pass.

proposedDagNode.title: Catalog conflict API enum schema guard
proposedDagNode.summary: Tighten the catalog conflict review API response schema so enum-backed fields reject arbitrary strings instead of only checking non-empty string shape.
deliverables: Enum-backed API response validators for catalog conflict review rows
Negative response fixture for arbitrary enum strings
Updated valid conflict review API fixtures
acceptanceCriteria: Catalog conflict API schema rejects invalid status, conflictKind, catalogSource, externalIdKind, and sourceRecordKind values.
A service or mock returning arbitrary strings for those fields fails a targeted API schema test.
Repository-backed valid conflict review responses keep passing API handler and server tests.
auditFocus: API contract drift from repository enums
Mocked conflict rows bypassing schema checks
